title: Acceptance tests do not start from an empty system; they share the globally-loaded dataset
evidence: test/acceptance/mcp_tools_test.exs:7-11 setup_all only ensures the app is started, and assertions depend on the full pre-loaded CSV data (e.g. :253 expects Flamengo atop 2019 Brasileirao). No per-scenario fixture seeding/teardown — contrary to the ATDD prompt's 'each scenario starts from a running but empty system and shares no data with other tests'.
suggestion: Seed a small fixture dataset per test (or inject a fresh DataStore) so scenarios are independent of the shipped CSVs and of each other.

title: Head-to-head win attribution uses substring matching, fragile for overlapping team names
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools/find_matches.ex:75-86 determines which side a team is on via String.contains?(downcased home, t1_down) rather than comparing normalized team identifiers; teams whose names are substrings of others could be misattributed.
suggestion: Attribute results by comparing TeamNormalizer-normalized identities instead of raw substring containment.

title: Match filtering supports season only, not an explicit date range
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools/find_matches.ex:7-14 and data_store.ex:265-266 filter by exact season; there is no from/to date-range parameter. R4 ('date range and/or season') is satisfied via season.
suggestion: Optionally add start_date/end_date args to find_matches for true date-range queries.

title: Per-row CSV parse errors are silently rescued and skipped
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer_mcp/data_store.ex:362-377 wraps each row in try/rescue _ -> :ok and rescues whole-file load failures to a warning, so malformed rows are dropped without count or surfaced metric.
suggestion: Count and log dropped rows per dataset so silent data loss is observable.
